message.content:
Search the web for "Anthropic Harness" to understand:
1. What is Anthropic Harness
2. How to configure it in a project
3. What files/configuration it requires

Also check if there's any existing harness configuration in the ~/dev folder by looking for .harness folders or harness-related files in other projects.

content.text: I'll search for information about Anthropic Harness and check for existing configurations in your projects.

input.description: Find all .harness directories in dev folder

content.content:
Web search results for query: "Anthropic Harness"

Links: [{"title":"Effective harnesses for long-running agents","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents"},{"title":"Introducing Claude Opus 4.5","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/news/claude-opus-4-5"},{"title":"Engineering \\ Anthropic","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering"},{"title":"Building agents with the Claude Agent SDK","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk"},{"title":"Agent harness capabilities - Docs by LangChain","url":"https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/deepagents/harness"},{"title":"Anthropic says it solved the long-running AI agent problem with a new multi-session Claude SDK | VentureBeat","url":"https://venturebeat.com/ai/anthropic-says-it-solved-the-long-running-ai-agent-problem-with-a-new-multi"},{"title":"Anthropic - Effective harnesses for long-running agents","url":"https://www.linkedin.com/posts/anthropicresearch_effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents-activity-7399550329031180288-xR_w"},{"title":"Anthropic cracks down on unauthorized Claude usage by third-party harnesses and rivals | VentureBeat","url":"https://venturebeat.com/technology/anthropic-cracks-down-on-unauthorized-claude-usage-by-third-party-harnesses"},{"title":"Demystifying evals for AI agents","url":"https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/demystifying-evals-for-ai-agents"},{"title":"Effective harnesses for long-running agents | Hacker News","url":"https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=46081704"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Anthropic Harness:

## Agent Harness

An agent harness is the system that enables a model to act as an agent: it processes inputs, orchestrates tool calls, and returns results.

## Claude Agent SDK

The Claude Agent SDK is a powerful, general-purpose agent harness adept at coding, as well as other tasks that require the model to use tools to gather context, plan, and execute. It has context management capabilities such as compaction, which enables an agent to work on a task without exhausting the context window.

## Long-Running Agent Harness

Anthropic has developed approaches for handling long-running agents that need to work across multiple context windows. Anthropic engineers proposed a two-fold approach for its Agent SDK: An initializer agent to set up the environment, and a coding agent to make incremental progress in each session and leave artifacts for the next.(no content)The updated Claude 4 prompting guide shared best practices for multi-context window workflows, including a harness structure that uses "a different prompt for the very first context window."

## Context and Use Cases

At Anthropic, the agent harness has begun to power almost all of their major agent loops, and the agent harness that powers Claude Code can power many other types of agents too.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

content.title: Building agents with the Claude Agent SDK
Agent harness capabilities - Docs by LangChain
Anthropic says it solved the long-running AI agent problem with a new multi-session Claude SDK | VentureBeat
Anthropic - Effective harnesses for long-running agents
Anthropic cracks down on unauthorized Claude usage by third-party harnesses and rivals | VentureBeat
Effective harnesses for long-running agents | Hacker News

## Agent Harness

content.content:
1â†’# Frontend Style Guide â€” Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2â†’
     3â†’Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4â†’This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5â†’
     6â†’> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. â˜•
     7â†’
     8â†’---
     9â†’
    10â†’## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11â†’- [Introduction](#introduction)
    12â†’- [Components & Screens â€” Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    13â†’- [Atomic Components â€” Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    14â†’- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    15â†’- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    16â†’- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    17â†’- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    18â†’- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    19â†’- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    20â†’- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    21â†’- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    22â†’- [Checklist](#checklist)
    23â†’
    24â†’---
    25â†’
    26â†’## Introduction
    27â†’
    28â†’The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    29â†’
    30â†’Key decisions:
    31â†’- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    32â†’- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    33â†’- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    34â†’- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    35â†’
    36â†’---
    37â†’
    38â†’## Components & Screens â€” Hook Component Pattern
    39â†’
    40â†’Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    41â†’
    42â†’```
    43â†’screens/Home/
    44â†’â”œâ”€â”€ index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    45â†’â”œâ”€â”€ view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    46â†’â””â”€â”€ Models/
    47â†’    â””â”€â”€ index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    48â†’```
    49â†’
    50â†’**Models/index.d.ts**
    51â†’```ts
    52â†’export interface HomeParams {
    53â†’  welcome?: string;
    54â†’}
    55â†’export interface HomeState {
    56â†’  greeting: string;
    57â†’  isLoading: boolean;
    58â†’}
    59â†’export interface HomeHandlers {
    60â†’  onPressCTA: () => void;
    61â†’}
    62â†’export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    63â†’```
    64â†’
    65â†’**index.ts (logic hook)**
    66â†’```ts
    67â†’import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from "react";
    68â†’import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from "./Models";
    69â†’import { useUserStore } from "@/services/user/store"; // Zustand slice example
    70â†’
    71â†’export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    72â†’  const name = useUserStore((s) => s.name);
    73â†’  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    74â†’
    75â†’  useEffect(() => {
    76â†’    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    77â†’    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    78â†’  }, []);
    79â†’
    80â†’  const greeting = useMemo(
    81â†’    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || "guest"}!`,
    82â†’    [params?.welcome, name]
    83â†’  );
    84â†’
    85â†’  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    86â†’    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    87â†’  }, []);
    88â†’
    89â†’  return { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA };
    90â†’}
    91â†’```
    92â†’
    93â†’**view.tsx (render)**
    94â†’```tsx
    95â†’import React from "react";
    96â†’import { View } from "react-native";
    97â†’import { Text, Button } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
    98â†’import { useHomeScreen } from "./index";
    99â†’import type { HomeParams } from "./Models";
   100â†’
   101â†’type Props = HomeParams;
   102â†’
   103â†’export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   104â†’  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   105â†’
   106â†’  if (isLoading) {
   107â†’    return (
   108â†’      <View>
   109â†’        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   110â†’      </View>
   111â†’    );
   112â†’  }
   113â†’
   114â†’  return (
   115â†’    <View>
   116â†’      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   117â†’      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   118â†’    </View>
   119â†’  );
   120â†’}
   121â†’```
   122â†’
   123â†’---
   124â†’
   125â†’## Atomic Components â€” Hook Component Pattern
   126â†’
   127â†’```
   128â†’atomic/atoms/Button/
   129â†’â”œâ”€â”€ index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
   130â†’â”œâ”€â”€ view.tsx        # render: export default Button
   131â†’â””â”€â”€ Models/
   132â†’    â””â”€â”€ index.d.ts
   133â†’```
   134â†’
   135â†’**Models/index.d.ts**
   136â†’```ts
   137â†’export interface ButtonProps {
   138â†’  text: string;
   139â†’  disabled?: boolean;
   140â†’  onPress?: () => void;
   141â†’}
   142â†’export interface ButtonState {
   143â†’  pressed: boolean;
   144â†’}
   145â†’export interface ButtonHandlers {
   146â†’  handlePress: () => void;
   147â†’}
   148â†’export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
   149â†’```
   150â†’
   151â†’**index.ts (logic hook)**
   152â†’```ts
   153â†’import { useCallback, useState } from "react";
   154â†’import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from "./Models";
   155â†’
   156â†’export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   157â†’  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   158â†’
   159â†’  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   160â†’    setPressed(true);
   161â†’    props.onPress?.();
   162â†’    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   163â†’  }, [props.onPress]);
   164â†’
   165â†’  return { pressed, handlePress };
   166â†’}
   167â†’```
   168â†’
   169â†’**view.tsx (render)**
   170â†’```tsx
   171â†’import React from "react";
   172â†’import { Pressable, View } from "react-native";
   173â†’import { Text } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
   174â†’import { useButton } from "./index";
   175â†’import type { ButtonProps } from "./Models";
   176â†’
   177â†’export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
   178â†’  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
   179â†’
   180â†’  return (
   181â†’    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
   182â†’      <View>
   183â†’        <Text>{props.text}{pressed ? " â€¢" : ""}</Text>
   184â†’      </View>
   185â†’    </Pressable>
   186â†’  );
   187â†’}
   188â†’```
   189â†’
   190â†’---
   191â†’
   192â†’## Project Organization
   193â†’
   194â†’```
   195â†’src/
   196â†’â”œâ”€â”€ @types/            # Global TS types
   197â†’â”œâ”€â”€ assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
   198â†’â”œâ”€â”€ atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
   199â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ atoms/
   200â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ molecules/
   201â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ organisms/
   202â†’â”œâ”€â”€ modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
   203â†’â”œâ”€â”€ screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
   204â†’â”œâ”€â”€ services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
   205â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
   206â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ class/         # Class with logical requests
   207â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ hooks/         # Specific service hooks
   208â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
   209â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ services/      # Business orchestration
   210â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
   211â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ store/         # Zustand slices (only)
   212â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ models/        # Domain types & contracts
   213â†’â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ utils/         # Reusable service helps
   214â†’â”‚   â””â”€â”€ index.ts       # Public exports
   215â†’â”œâ”€â”€ hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
   216â†’â”œâ”€â”€ routes/            # Navigation config
   217â†’â”œâ”€â”€ settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
   218â†’â””â”€â”€ utils/             # General-purpose helpers
   219â†’```
   220â†’
   221â†’> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
   222â†’
   223â†’---
   224â†’
   225â†’## Services Architecture
   226â†’
   227â†’**Flow:** `UI â†’ services â†’ repository â†’ api` and `UI â†’ services â†’ store (Zustand)`
   228â†’
   229â†’- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.
   230â†’- **class/**: class with logical requests.
   231â†’- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   232â†’- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.
   233â†’- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.
   234â†’- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.
   235â†’- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).
   236â†’- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.
   237â†’- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   238â†’
   239â†’Principles:
   240â†’- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   241â†’- No circular dependencies.
   242â†’- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   243â†’
   244â†’---
   245â†’
   246â†’## State Management (Zustand-only)
   247â†’
   248â†’- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.
   249â†’- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.
   250â†’- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.
   251â†’- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   252â†’
   253â†’**Slice example**
   254â†’```ts
   255â†’import type { StateCreator } from "zustand";
   256â†’import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from "../models/store";
   257â†’
   258â†’export type AuthSlice = {
   259â†’    auth: AuthState;
   260â†’    authActions: AuthActions
   261â†’};
   262â†’
   263â†’const initialAuth = {
   264â†’    user: null,
   265â†’    loading: false,
   266â†’    error: undefined,
   267â†’}
   268â†’
   269â†’export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<AuthSlice, [], [], AuthSlice> = (set) => ({
   270â†’    auth: initialAuth,
   271â†’    authActions: {
   272â†’        setLoading: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, isLoading: v } })),
   273â†’        setUser: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, user: v } })),
   274â†’        setError: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, error: v } })),
   275â†’        reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   276â†’    }
   277â†’});
   278â†’```
   279â†’
   280â†’**Root Store**
   281â†’
   282â†’- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps â€” including SuperApp.
   283â†’
   284â†’```ts
   285â†’import { create } from "zustand";
   286â†’import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from "zustand/middleware";
   287â†’import { immer } from "zustand/middleware/immer";
   288â†’
   289â†’import { createUserSlice, type UserSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user";
   290â†’import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-yourmf";
   291â†’
   292â†’import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   293â†’
   294â†’/** Add your slice Type */
   295â†’export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   296â†’
   297â†’export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   298â†’  devtools(
   299â†’    persist(
   300â†’      immer((...a) => ({
   301â†’        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   302â†’        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   303â†’      })),
   304â†’      {
   305â†’        name: "root-storage",
   306â†’        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   307â†’        partialize: (s) => ({
   308â†’          user: s.user,
   309â†’          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   310â†’        }),
   311â†’        merge: (persisted, current) => ({ ...current, ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>) }),
   312â†’      }
   313â†’    ),
   314â†’    { name: "RootStore" }
   315â†’  )
   316â†’);
   317â†’```
   318â†’
   319â†’- For Use on Class.
   320â†’
   321â†’```ts
   322â†’class Auth {
   323â†’  private readonly setAuth;
   324â†’
   325â†’  constructor () {
   326â†’    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
   327â†’  }
   328â†’
   329â†’  function doSomething(data) {
   330â†’    this.setUser({ ...data });
   331â†’  }
   332â†’}
   333â†’```
   334â†’
   335â†’- For Use on Component/Screen.
   336â†’- UI never imports the store directly â€” uses selectors
   337â†’
   338â†’```ts
   339â†’const loading = useRootStore((s) => s.user.isLoading);
   340â†’const token = useRootStore((s) => s.auth.token);
   341â†’```
   342â†’
   343â†’---
   344â†’
   345â†’## Routes Naming
   346â†’
   347â†’Standardize as **`Acronym + Route`**:
   348â†’- âœ… `SPPayment` (Sem Parar Payment)
   349â†’- âœ… `FTHPayment` (Fintech Payment)
   350â†’
   351â†’---
   352â†’
   353â†’## Folder Naming Rules
   354â†’
   355â†’| Folder                              | Convention   | Examples                    |
   356â†’|-------------------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------|
   357â†’| `atomic`, `screens`, `routes`       | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `CarDetails`  |
   358â†’| `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, etc. | `camelCase`  | `useAuth`, `authService`    |
   359â†’
   360â†’---
   361â†’
   362â†’## Code Standards
   363â†’
   364â†’**Language**: English only.  
   365â†’**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).
   366â†’
   367â†’**Explanatory variables**
   368â†’```js
   369â†’const onlyNumbersRegex = /^\d+$/;
   370â†’const getNumberValidation = (message) => (value) => !onlyNumbersRegex.test(value) && message;
   371â†’const isNumber = getNumberValidation("error message");
   372â†’isNumber(1);
   373â†’```
   374â†’
   375â†’**Semantic constants**
   376â†’```js
   377â†’const DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS = 86400000;
   378â†’setTimeout(doSomething, DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS);
   379â†’```
   380â†’
   381â†’**Library workarounds with link**
   382â†’```js
   383â†’// Refer to issue https://github.com/ngokevin/react-file-reader-input/issues/11#issuecomment-363484861
   384â†’// eslint-disable-next-line no-param-reassign
   385â†’event.target.value = "";
   386â†’```
   387â†’
   388â†’**JSDoc**
   389â†’```js
   390â†’/**
   391â†’ * Truncates an event name to the max length.
   392â†’ * @param {*} eventName
   393â†’ * @param {*} maxLength
   394â†’ */
   395â†’```
   396â†’
   397â†’**Hooks: multi-line deps & IIFE**
   398â†’```ts
   399â†’useEffect(() => {
   400â†’  (async () => {
   401â†’    await CustomMath.randomSum();
   402â†’    setSum(42);
   403â†’  })();
   404â†’}, [
   405â†’  setSum,
   406â†’]);
   407â†’```
   408â†’
   409â†’**Extract conditions**
   410â†’```ts
   411â†’const hasSelectedOffer = !!(selectedProduct || selectedLeasingOffer);
   412â†’if (!hasSelectedOffer && !individualOffer) return;
   413â†’```
   414â†’
   415â†’**Optional chaining**
   416â†’```ts
   417â†’const answers = formikForm?.values?.answers;
   418â†’```
   419â†’
   420â†’**JSX indentation**
   421â†’```tsx
   422â†’{
   423â†’  !isEnd && (
   424â†’    <Button text="CTA" onPress={handleNavigate} />
   425â†’  )
   426â†’}
   427â†’```
   428â†’
   429â†’**Early returns**
   430â†’```ts
   431â†’if (!data) return null;
   432â†’return <View />;
   433â†’```
   434â†’
   435â†’**Nullish coalescing**
   436â†’```ts
   437â†’return data ?? 0;
   438â†’```
   439â†’---
   440â†’
   441â†’## Styles Patterns
   442â†’
   443â†’- Do **not** use `StyleSheet` or `styled-components`.
   444â†’- Use **@semparar/design-system-app** for components, tokens, and theming.
   445â†’
   446â†’---
   447â†’
   448â†’## Cache Pattern
   449â†’
   450â†’Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   451â†’
   452â†’```ts
   453â†’import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   454â†’
   455â†’export interface CachedData<T> {
   456â†’  timestamp: number;
   457â†’  data: T;
   458â†’}
   459â†’
   460â†’export interface StorageContract<T> {
   461â†’  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   462â†’  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   463â†’  remove(): Promise<void>;
   464â†’}
   465â†’
   466â†’export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   467â†’  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   468â†’
   469â†’  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   470â†’    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   471â†’    if (!json) return null;
   472â†’    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   473â†’  }
   474â†’
   475â†’  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   476â†’    const payload: CachedData<T> = { timestamp: Date.now(), data };
   477â†’    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   478â†’  }
   479â†’
   480â†’  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   481â†’    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   482â†’  }
   483â†’}
   484â†’```
   485â†’
   486â†’**Guidelines**
   487â†’- One storage class per module/feature.
   488â†’- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
   489â†’- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
   490â†’- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
   491â†’- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.
   492â†’
   493â†’---
   494â†’
   495â†’## Checklist
   496â†’
   497â†’- [ ] Screens/Components follow **Hook Component pattern** (`index.ts` hook + `view.tsx` render)
   498â†’- [ ] Types live in **Models/**
   499â†’- [ ] Only **Zustand** for global/shared state under **/services/<domain>/store/**
   500â†’- [ ] UI never calls API directly (go through **/services**)
   501â†’- [ ] Code standards and styles patterns applied
   502â†’- [ ] Cache implemented via **GenericStorage** (when needed)
   503â†’- [ ] Routes and folder naming conventions followed
   504â†’
   505â†’---
   506â†’
   507â†’Happy shipping! ðŸš€

<system-reminder>

But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.
</system-reminder>

file.content:
# SPMAIS-33081 - Airline Miles MktPlace Redemption (Webview)

## Objective
Implement redirect to Vertem's Airline Miles MktPlace WebView when the customer selects "Airline Miles" on the Points Program screen.

## Reference
Follow the MktPlace store implementation (SPMAIS-29672). The only difference is using `catalog=MILEAGE`.

---

## API

**Endpoint:**
```
GET /marketplace/v1/vertem/getToken?redirectTo=MARKETPLACE&catalog=MILEAGE
```

**Headers:** (automatically filled by the app mechanism)
- x-api-key, uuid, empresa, codigocanal, appversion, user-agent, devicename, lat, long, hash, accesstoken, codigocliente

**Response:**
```json
{
  "dados": {
    "redirectURL": "https://semparar-middleware-sso-hml.vertem.com/vertem/auth?..."
  },
  "status": {
    "codigoRetorno": 0,
    "mensagem": "Sucesso"
  }
}
```

---

## Features (Checklist)

### 1. Service Layer - Airline Miles
- [ ] Create `src/services/airlineMiles/api/index.ts` - HTTP Client
- [ ] Create `src/services/airlineMiles/repository/index.ts` - Data mapping
- [ ] Create `src/services/airlineMiles/services/index.ts` - Orchestration
- [ ] Create `src/services/airlineMiles/store/index.ts` - Zustand slice
- [ ] Create `src/services/airlineMiles/models/index.ts` - TypeScript types
- [ ] Create `src/services/airlineMiles/hooks/index.ts` - Consumer hook
- [ ] Export in `src/services/airlineMiles/index.ts`

### 2. WebView Screen
- [ ] Create `src/screens/AirlineMilesWebView/index.tsx` - Hook logic
- [ ] Create `src/screens/AirlineMilesWebView/view.tsx` - WebView rendering
- [ ] Create `src/screens/AirlineMilesWebView/Models/index.ts` - Types
- [ ] Reuse pattern from `src/screens/RewardProgramMarketplace/`

### 3. Navigation
- [ ] Add `AirlineMilesWebView` route in `src/routes/Models/index.ts`
- [ ] Register screen in `src/routes/index.tsx`
- [ ] Update `useCardBuilderWhereUseMyPoints` to navigate when clicking "Airline Miles"

### 4. Analytics (Tagging)
- [ ] Implement `eventScreenViewV3` when entering the screen
- [ ] Implement `eventSelectContent` when clicking "Airline Miles"

---

## Reference Files

| File | Purpose |
|------|---------|
| `src/screens/RewardProgramMarketplace/` | WebView screen template |
| `src/redux/sagas/marketplace/getMarketplace.ts` | API call logic (reference) |
| `src/hooks/custom/useCardBuilderWhereUseMyPoints/index.ts` | Navigation hook to modify |
| `src/screens/Points/data/index.ts` | `KEY_NAME_TO_ICON.AIRLINE_MILES` mapping |
| `src/services/additionalCard/` | Service layer example with Zustand |
| `.harness/docs/1.Frontend_Style_Guide.md` | Mandatory style guide |

---

## Mandatory Rules

1. **Use Zustand** - DO NOT use Redux/Sagas for new code
2. **Hook Component Pattern** - Separate `index.tsx` (logic) from `view.tsx` (UI)
3. **Types in Models/** - Separate interfaces for Props, State, Return
4. **Code in English** - Names, comments, documentation
5. **Data flow**: UI â†’ hooks â†’ services â†’ repository â†’ api

---

## Final Folder Structure

```
src/
â”œâ”€â”€ services/
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ airlineMiles/
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ api/index.ts
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ repository/index.ts
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ services/index.ts
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ store/index.ts
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ models/index.ts
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ hooks/index.ts
â”‚       â””â”€â”€ index.ts
â”œâ”€â”€ screens/
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ AirlineMilesWebView/
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ index.tsx
â”‚       â”œâ”€â”€ view.tsx
â”‚       â””â”€â”€ Models/index.ts
â””â”€â”€ routes/
    â”œâ”€â”€ Models/index.ts (update)
    â””â”€â”€ index.tsx (update)
```

---

## Final Validation

- [ ] Screen opens WebView with correct Vertem URL
- [ ] Analytics fires correct events
- [ ] Back navigation works
- [ ] Loading state works
- [ ] Error state works
- [ ] No TypeScript errors
- [ ] Followed Frontend_Style_Guide.md

content.text: Now let me fetch the official Anthropic Harness documentation article to get more details:

input.prompt: What is an agent harness and what are the key components and best practices for configuring it?

content.content:
# Agent Harness: Components and Best Practices

An agent harness is a framework that enables AI models to work on complex tasks across multiple context windows. Based on Anthropic's research, here are the key components:

## Core Architecture

The harness uses a two-part solution:

1. **Initializer Agent**: "The very first agent session uses a specialized prompt that asks the model to set up the initial environment: an `init.sh` script, a claude-progress.txt file that keeps a log of what agents have done, and an initial git commit that shows what files were added."

2. **Coding Agent**: Subsequent sessions focus on incremental progress with structured updates and clean code states.

## Key Components

**Feature List**: A JSON file containing detailed feature descriptions, all initially marked as incomplete, preventing premature project conclusion.

**Progress Tracking**: Documentation and git history allowing agents to quickly understand project state upon starting new sessions.

**Testing Infrastructure**: Browser automation tools and end-to-end verification processes.

**Environment Setup**: An initialization script enabling consistent server startup across sessions.

## Best Practices

- Start each session by reviewing progress files and git logs
- Work on one feature at a time rather than attempting comprehensive solutions
- Verify functionality through user-like interactions, not just code analysis
- Maintain clean, mergeable code after each session
- Use git commits to document progress and enable rollback capabilities

This approach mirrors effective human engineering practices adapted for long-horizon autonomous work.

# Agent Harness: Components and Best Practices

An agent harness is a framework that enables AI models to work on complex tasks across multiple context windows. Based on Anthropic's research, here are the key components:

## Core Architecture

The harness uses a two-part solution:

1. **Initializer Agent**: "The very first agent session uses a specialized prompt that asks the model to set up the initial environment: an `init.sh` script, a claude-progress.txt file that keeps a log of what agents have done, and an initial git commit that shows what files were added."

2. **Coding Agent**: Subsequent sessions focus on incremental progress with structured updates and clean code states.

## Key Components

**Feature List**: A JSON file containing detailed feature descriptions, all initially marked as incomplete, preventing premature project conclusion.

**Progress Tracking**: Documentation and git history allowing agents to quickly understand project state upon starting new sessions.

**Testing Infrastructure**: Browser automation tools and end-to-end verification processes.

**Environment Setup**: An initialization script enabling consistent server startup across sessions.

## Best Practices

- Start each session by reviewing progress files and git logs
- Work on one feature at a time rather than attempting comprehensive solutions
- Verify functionality through user-like interactions, not just code analysis
- Maintain clean, mergeable code after each session
- Use git commits to document progress and enable rollback capabilities

This approach mirrors effective human engineering practices adapted for long-horizon autonomous work.

content.text:
## Summary

I've completed a comprehensive research on Anthropic Harness and analyzed your existing configurations. Here are my findings:

### What is Anthropic Harness?

An **Anthropic Harness** is a framework that enables AI agents (like Claude) to work on complex, long-running projects across multiple context windows. It's not a commercial product for fintech apps, but rather an internal system Anthropic developed for managing multi-session agent workflows.

**Key Components:**
1. **Initializer Agent** - Sets up the initial environment with setup scripts and progress tracking
2. **Coding Agent** - Handles incremental progress in subsequent sessions
3. **Feature List** - JSON file tracking incomplete features
4. **Progress Tracking** - Documentation and git history for context between sessions
5. **Environment Setup** - Scripts for consistent initialization

### Your Existing Configuration

Your project at `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/.harness` already has a complete harness setup with:

- **`.harness/CLAUDE.md`** - Root rules file pointing to documentation
- **`.harness/docs/PROJECT.md`** - Project metadata (superapp architecture, TypeScript/linter notes)
- **`.harness/docs/STYLE_GUIDE.md`** - Comprehensive Zustand-only architecture guide (507 lines)
- **`.harness/SPMAIS-33081-CASHBACK-MILHAS-WEBVIEW/CLAUDE.md`** - Task-specific instructions for the Airline Miles WebView feature

### Current Status of /Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-rewardprogram

âœ— **No .harness directory exists** - The rewardprogram project is not yet configured

### What You Need to Do

To configure the harness for the rewardprogram project, you need to create:

```
.harness/
â”œâ”€â”€ CLAUDE.md (root instructions)
â”œâ”€â”€ docs/
â”‚   â”œâ”€â”€ PROJECT.md (project metadata)
â”‚   â””â”€â”€ STYLE_GUIDE.md (architecture guide)
â””â”€â”€ [TASK-FOLDER]/
    â””â”€â”€ CLAUDE.md (task-specific instructions)
```

### Key Architecture Standards (from STYLE_GUIDE.md)

Your projects follow the **Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern**:

- **State Management**: Zustand slices only (no Redux/Sagas for new code)
- **Component Pattern**: Logic hook (`index.ts`) + View component (`view.tsx`)
- **Services Architecture**: `UI â†’ services â†’ repository â†’ api`
- **Project Structure**: `/atomic`, `/screens`, `/services`, `/routes`, `/hooks`, `/utils`
- **Storage**: GenericStorage class with typed cache
- **Naming**: `camelCase` for files/functions, `PascalCase` for components/classes

### Sources:
- [Effective harnesses for long-running agents](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/effective-harnesses-for-long-running-agents)
- [Building agents with the Claude Agent SDK](https://www.anthropic.com/engineering/building-agents-with-the-claude-agent-sdk)
- Existing configuration in `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/.harness/`
